characters.name: 105E
characters.description: 105EBiographical informationGenderFemale programmingOccupationHelper droidVoiced byDiane MorganRelationsFriendsYadveer(mutualite)FrankieAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Idiosyncratic Induction""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" You proper get on my nerves—and I don't even have any. 105Eis a helper droid at theHive. She is sent byThe Headto retrieve Frankie and bring her to a lecture after her arrival, and later helps Frankie attempt to showthe rockto the Head by using her mutualiteYadveer'sability to project memories. She makes her first appearance inIdiosyncratic Induction, and is voiced by comedianDiane Morgan. 105E is dry-witted, mischievous, work-shy, blunt, and somewhat gregarious at times (usually with other droids). When not busy with whatever work is assigned to her, 105E seems to spend most of her free time doing leisure activities and pastimes like going to parties, socializing or playing games such as badminton. Although not very friendly or affectionate, 105E does show appreciation for Yadveer and even some friendliness towards Frankie.

characters.name: Dugg
characters.description: DuggBiographical informationSpeciesAlienGenderMaleAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 3""Diminishing Discourse" Duggis an alien inhabitant of theCentrium, whomElliottthrows a goodbye party for after deciding to do a good deed for a stranger inParallel Paradox.  Dugg is aloof and appears somewhat irritable at times, as well as seemingly insecure about his true height. He also is shown to be at least somewhat antisocial, and doesn't seem to enjoy being the center of attention much. Despite these traits, he does have a kind and tolerant side to his personality, as shown in his effort to attend the party Elliott organized for him inParallel Paradoxbecause of how much Elliott seemed to care about it.

characters.name: Elliott
characters.description: ElliottBiographical informationAliasThe earthlingThe anomalyKiddo (byFrankie)Archibald Trombone Jr. (together withMo)Planet of originEarthAge11SpeciesHumanGenderMaleOccupationStudent at the HiveVoiced bySamuel FaraciRelationsFamilyFrankie(mother)Aunt Mo (Unseen, only spoken of)Unnamed motherFriendsMo(Best friend)NaraPrestonAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 1""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" We're in space—things could be entirely different up here. Elliott[Note 1]is the titular protagonist of the Cartoon Network showElliott from Earth. He is an eleven-year-old human fromEarthwho lives on a space station called theCentrium, along with his motherFrankieand his dinosaur companionMo. He makes his first appearance inWednesday Part 1and is voiced bySamuel Faraci. Elliott has a strong curiosity and enthusiasm for adventure, and is always willing to try out a new idea thanks to his open-mindedness and his readiness to explore. He sees things with a perspective that is very optimistic, excitable, and flexible. He often has a very fast-paced and direct thought process, and tends to think of things in a straightforward or even idealistic way, but his thought process still has a good amount of nuance overall and isn't shallow or dull. Elliott is typically excitable, eager, adventurous, and quick to act. Although he is not entirely reckless or unthinking, and tends to be quite attentive to his goals even as they go in unexpected directions, his eagerness to act means he’ll often leap into a new situation or put an idea into action without fully considering the possible results besides whatever he has already presumed. Generally Elliott is very action-oriented in his approach to things, being dedicated to whatever idea or task he may have and driven to resolve it. With his excitable nature, he often seems to live in the moment, and typically thinks and takes action in a naturally fast-paced (though usually not sloppy or impatient) way. These traits can occasionally lead him to get ahead of himself in his thinking or reactions too, so he may sometimes view something as more significant or problematic than it really is. However, Elliott is also very intuitive and perceptive. He's quick to notice things about a new situation and to put together different ideas or details, and he can swiftly see the bigger picture or "fill in the gaps" between observations to figure something new out. This trait helps to shape Elliott's creative and eager problem-solving, allowing him to quickly see the big picture of a situation and then put a plan together using that knowledge. Elliott's perceptiveness sometimes combines with his instinct, to warn him of what may be going on underneath the surface in some situations, such as in the events ofWednesday Part 4. As a result of his previous nomadic lifestyle on Earth, Elliott can be both somewhat more childish and somewhat more mature than his age. While occasionally he can have the naivety or the playfulness

characters.name: Frankie
characters.description: FrankieBiographical informationAliasMom (Elliott)The anomalyPlanet of originEarthAge36SpeciesHumanGenderFemaleOccupationGeologistVoiced byNaomi McDonaldRelationsFamilyElliott(son)Unnamed wifeFriendsMoMrs. Argolis105EAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 1""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" I believe this is evidence that intelligent life traveled to our planet at the time of the dinosaurs; intelligent life of extraterrestrial origin. Dr.Frankie[Note 1]is the tritagonist ofElliott from Earth. She is a thirty-six-year-old geologist fromEarthandElliott'smother. She makes her first appearance inWednesday Part 1and is voiced byNaomi McDonald. Frankie, much like Elliott, is very inquisitive and has an enthusiasm for discovery. This makes her very dedicated and ambitious, and motivates her to continuously investigate the mysterious meteorite even after failure or rejection. On her and Elliott's adventure into the Centrium, she often asks questions and theorizes answers about their encounters in the strange new place, wondering about the workings of the alien environments and wanting to know more. Frankie's ambition is a key trait of hers, as it motivates her to study the meteorite for years and to constantly search for answers and information about it, always with certainty that she is right about it being worth investigating even despite the tolls on her and Elliott's lifestyle that it takes. Frankie has a very logical and rational mind. In new situations, she often analyzes things carefully, has a more in-depth and realistic thought process, and usually thinks scientifically to come up with solutions to her problems. However, she does not entirely lack impulsiveness: when driven by her excitement about finding answers, Frankie may rush into a situation, or jump to conclusions about something before ensuring that her ideas are logical. Towards others, Frankie is usually somewhat formal but also acts with a calm friendliness. Though she is not as actively outgoing as Elliott andMotend to be, she is still open to meeting and talking with others around the Centrium. As she continues to settle there, she often appears to strive to make a good first impression on neighbors and acquaintances. Frankie's relationship with Elliott is perhaps the most important one in her life. She is very close to Elliott and trusts him deeply, and their dynamic is based on mutual respect and teamwork after having worked together throughout their nomadic lifestyle for years before. As an authority figure, Frankie steps up when it is needed but is still always gentle, fair, and rather flexible. She is often somewhat protective toward Elliott, especially as they go into the unknown and face potential danger when first arriving on the Centrium. However, she is not overly protective, and still trusts that he can take care of himself in everyday situations, with the two of them sometimes even mutually relying on each other's help.

characters.name: Gym
characters.description: GymBiographical informationAliasGymnasiumGenderFemaleOccupationTeacher at theHiveAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Inadvertent Inversion""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" Gymnasium, otherwise known as Gym, is an alien inhabitant of theCentriumand a teacher at theHive. She makes her first appearance inInadvertent Inversion, teaching a class on different species' cuisines and dining. Gym is a large, bulky alien species with pink skin and a few darker pink spots. At the bottom of her large head are many short, tentacle-like protrusions that sit over her body, with the ability to produce a flavorful, edible substance. Gym's face includes two narrow, yellow eyes that protrude out from the head with a thick unibrow over both of them, and a large mouth with purple lips. She wears a typical "lunch lady" outfit with a soda jerk hat, a white shirt with a small blue line on the right side of the chest, white gloves, a pale tan apron around her waist, and short white shoes. Gym acts in a strict but fair way as a teacher, with a very direct and somewhat monotone way of speaking. She is shown to be professional and calm even in chaotic situations, maintaining order and overseeing all of the students in her class. Despite a generally blunt, contained, and unchanging demeanor, Gym does also seem to encourage boldness, openness and dedication in her students as she teaches new subjects to them.

characters.name: Hive Director
characters.description: Hive DirectorBiographical informationAgeMiddle agedGenderFemaleOccupationHive directorVoiced byAlyssa Darwin SachexRelationsFamilyUnnamed husband, unnamed sonFriendsElliott,FrankieAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 3""Memory Mayhem" TheHive Directoris the director of operations at theHive, who handles and oversees fields such as finances, technology, and employment there. She makes her first appearance inIdiosyncratic Induction. Her species notably ages "in reverse", making her appear to be a young child even though she is actually much older. The Hive Director is friendly and welcoming to new arrivals, and has a very polite, patient, and peaceable demeanor as director at the Hive. She embraces the wide diversity of species on the Centrium, as well as the wide range of knowledge that they bring to the Hive, and believes that there is no such thing as "normal" there.

characters.name: Kane
characters.description: KaneBiographical informationGenderMaleOccupationMaintenance workerAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 3""Wednesday Part 3" Kane, along with his other headBrett, is a two-headed maintenance worker on theCentrium. Kane works in the tunnels beneathCentrium City, where he monitors the status of biospheres there such asBiosphere 1138and the maintenance droids within. Kane helpsElliott,FrankieandMoout of Biosphere 1138 and into the city above, working to sneak them past the security scanner in the tunnels. Kane and Brett are a tall, two-headed alien with a green body, darker green spots, two arms and three legs. Kane, the lower head, is positioned on the chest, and has a defined brow ridge, red-tinted eyes, and flat nostrils. Brett, the higher head, has wide yellow eyes atop eyestalks, flat nostrils, and a large mouth that usually sits in a frown shape. Brett notably only speaks in "mah" sounds, unlike Kane, who is able to understand the meaning of these sounds. Kane is rather gruff, seemingly lazy or irresponsible, and somewhat self-centered or uncharitable beyond whatever is necessary. However, he also shows cleverness and wit in his ability to find a way to sneak Elliott, Frankie and Mo out of the maintenance tunnels while avoiding trouble with his boss. Though Brett doesn't show as much personality since he doesn't speak like Kane does, he does share this cleverness with Kane as he helps in creating and performing the plan. Brett is also later noted to be "the kind one" of the two after he convinces Kane to give the earthlings a pack of snack food so that they won't be sent off into the city with nothing.

characters.name: Lord Kallous the Merciless
characters.description: Lord Kallous the MercilessBiographical informationAliasMr. K (Hive Director,Elliott,Mo)GenderMaleOccupationSchoolteacherIntergalactic tyrant (former)Voiced byDavid WarnerRelationsEnemiesElliottMoHis studentsPrince CryonAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Idiosyncratic Induction""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" Lord Kallous the Mercilessis a secondary character in theCartoon NetworkseriesElliott from Earth. A former intergalactic tyrant who had partially caused the destruction of his homeworld, he is currently a schoolteacher on theCentriumat theHive.ElliottandMoare in his class. Kallous is an adult male alien with a green muscular body and a two-horned skull-like head. His face includes an open nasal cavity like that of a skull, sharp teeth, and black eyes with orange pupils. He wears a dark red hood with a cape, dark red gloves, two red sashes with a diamond emblem in the middle, a maroon belt with a silver and gold buckle, red pants, and tall gray boots with spikes on them. He usually carries around a long brown staff with sharp claws holding a orange orb. Kallous is often angry, intimidating, aggressive or short-tempered, and as a former warlord he acts stereotypically evil in many ways. He also tends to be rather exhausted and displeased at having to work as a teacher at the Hive, and usually is easily irritated by his students. Kallous tends to be somewhat cruel, as he enjoys giving punishments to students and often laments his inability to be more barbaric with it. He also appears to often miss his past as a warlord and his battles against his former nemesisPrince Cryon.

characters.name: Mo
characters.description: MoBiographical informationAliasThe green one (byKane)Archibald Trombone Jr. (together withElliott)Planet of originEarth(biologically)SpeciesStegosaurusGenderMaleOccupationHive StudentVoiced byNoah Kaye BentleyRelationsFriendsElliott(best friend)FrankieNaraPrestonAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 2""Diminishing Discourse" Wait, does that mean there are other me's on your planet? Mois the deuteragonist of theCartoon NetworkseriesElliott from Earth, serving asElliott'strusted companion and best friend. First appearing inWednesday Part 2, he is aStegosauruswho has lived stranded onBiosphere 1138as long as he can remember. Because of his poor eyesight, he typically wears a pair of glasses originally owned byFrankie. Mo is kind, honest, loyal, naive, gentle, and usually easygoing. He can be somewhat nervous or reserved at times, but overall is typically much more curious and open. As he starts to explore the Centrium with Elliott and Frankie, he is able to open up more, and becomes a bit more eager and energetic. Similarly to Elliott, Mo is usually very willing to explore something new if the opportunity arises. Mo is very loyal and honest; he values honesty deeply, and is even shown to be very upset even by unintentionally lying. Mo also generally speaks and acts in a very open and sincere way, and his loyalty combined with his good-hearted personality means he makes a genuine and friendly connection to almost everyone he meets. However, his traits of loyalty and honesty are at their strongest in his relationships with Frankie and Elliott, and he is extremely loyal to them both. Mo's loyalty also means that he is very dedicated to following any guidelines or instructions given to him (unless he has a good reason not to). He also tends to be very agreeable, and will often easily accept propositions or commands from others. Mo has a very straightforward and lighthearted way of thinking. His thought process is usually very simple, and is heavily based upon whatever knowledge, goals, or expectations he already has in his mind. This trait can lead him to seem somewhat dramatic or unpredictable at times, as he often simply acts like the "role" that fits whatever he expects or knows about the situation and what he should do in it, even if those ideas change quickly. Mo often doesn't consider other possibilities or concepts as he thinks about something, but sometimes this straightforward way of thinking can lead him to a solution or a helpful perspective that others never thought of. Mo's naivety is a prominent part of his character. It is somewhat because of his lack of experience or knowledge due to living in Biosphere 1138 for as long as he remembers, but it also seems to somewhat be a natural part of his personality. However, Mo is also a fast learner, so he very quickly and unquestioningly accepts new ideas or explanations that are told to him. Because of this, he is also able to easily let go of these things if they end up bein

characters.name: Mrs. Argolis
characters.description: Mrs. ArgolisBiographical informationSpeciesAlienGenderFemaleRelationsFriendsFrankie(neighbor)Elliott(neighbor)AppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Developing Dilemma""Diminishing Discourse" Mrs. ArgolisisElliottandFrankie’s three-headed next-door neighbor. She usually has a sour or fussy attitude and a habit of making rude remarks, as well as a tendency to complain at any problem or inconvenience. However, after their initial meeting, she soon becomes somewhat more tolerant and kind toward her neighbors. Her three heads appear to sometimes bicker with each other. Mrs. Argolis is a purple-skinned alien with three heads, each one on a long neck branching out from her body. Each head has one eye and eyebrow. Her leftmost head has light brown hair, wears a white headband and round white earrings, and has orange lips and freckles around her cheeks. Her middle head has brown hair, golden hoop earrings and blue lips. Her rightmost head has reddish-brown hair, green lips, blush around her cheeks, and small earrings consisting of two golden spheres and a triangular ornament. Her body is large and rounded, and she wears a yellow dress with short sleeves, white trim at the bottom, and a circular white and blue button at the waist, as well as white shoes with heels. Mrs. Argolis is oftentimes very brash, picky, and even hostile towards others, with a tendency to make rude or passive-aggressive remarks from all three of her heads. She can also be somewhat argumentative and act demanding or accusatory toward others at times, and sometimes her three heads may even argue amongst themselves. Mrs. Argolis also tends to complain often about any inconvenience, or bluntly point out any flaws or imperfections in something. Despite this nature and her oftentimes rude behavior, she later is seen being more open and tolerant towards others and becomes kinder toward her neighbors, although her overall fussy personality remains.

characters.name: Nara
characters.description: NaraBiographical informationGenderFemaleOccupationStudentRelationsFriendsElliottMoAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Idiosyncratic Induction""Diminishing Discourse" Narais a classmate ofElliottandMo. Her species notably has the ability to predict the future around them, both to envision immediate occurrences nearby and to predict what will happen if a certain imminent plan or event occurs. Nara typically has a dry and apathetic demeanor, though, as this superintelligent foresight means she has quickly grown bored of constantly knowing everything that will happen around her. Her species can also trade this power, transferring it through contact during a handshake, in exchange for an ability or talent of some sort from whoever they are trading with. Nara often acts very apathetic or calm, mostly due to her ability to see the future and know everything that's going to happen next. Despite this, she also has a somewhat sarcastic and mischievous side on occasion. Overall, Nara is still friendly and casual in demeanor, and shows some generosity towards others as well. When temporarily without her ability to see the future after trading it to Elliott and Mo, Nara became much more energetic and enthusiastic about everyday life, since she was thrilled to experience what it was like to not know what would happen next. Ultimately she still was willing to take her power back, knowing that she needed it and that Elliott and Mo were struggling with it.

characters.name: Noam
characters.description: NoamBiographical informationAliasLinguistics (byThe Head)GenderMaleOccupationLinguistics specialist within the HeadPart-time worker at the Emporium of Wonders storeVoiced byGyles BrandrethAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Memory Mayhem""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" Noamis a recurring character inElliott from Earth. An eccentric linguistics expert and part-time customer service worker, with the ability to recreate objects based on others' memories, Noam is one of the many aliens that make upThe Head. He makes his first appearance inMemory Mayhem, and is voiced byGyles Brandreth. Noam is a tall adult alien with a round blue body, four long tentacle-like limbs and two similar arms, a moustache-like pair of barbels on his face, and two yellow eyes on stalks above his head. Noam's body is semi-transparent, and appears to have a somewhat liquid or gelatinous consistency inside. Noam typically wears a red bowtie. Noam is very formal, welcoming, and well-educated, with a polite but eccentric personality. He usually acts very helpful and open, and seems to enjoy being able to assist others. Noam also shows some academic curiosity in his fascination with linguistics, knowledge of different alien languages and the workings of thetranslation gas, and dedication to many different hobbies and areas of study. Despite being quite knowledgeable overall, he can be prone to confidently making mistakes in his understanding of other species—for example, continuously misusing nonsense phrases in English, or incorrectly guessingElliott's gender during their first meeting.

characters.name: Preston
characters.description: PrestonYoungBabyTeenOlder teenThirtiesFortiesOld manElderlyBiographical informationGenderMaleOccupationStudentRelationsFriendsElliottMoAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Idiosyncratic Induction""Temporal Tedium" Prestonis a classmate ofElliottandMo. He talks and moves extremely slowly, due to thenecklacehe wears to slow his species' rapid aging process and overall experience of time. He is also able to regenerate once he reaches the end of his life cycle. Typically, Preston appears as a small and compact round alien, with a body that looks somewhat similar to a green slime ball and includes a long, hairstyle-like protrusion atop his head. His face includes two small, closed eyes, one eyebrow ridge, and a small mouth. He wears red and white shoes and, most of the time, a round light blue necklace with an infinity symbol on it. Preston typically acts very in a very slow, mild and simple way, due to the effect of the time-slowing necklace he wears. Although he seems dull or boring due to this, under the surface Preston can be very skilled, lively, and outgoing, as seen when he takes the necklace off and is able to move and speak at regular speed. Preston is also rather clever and mischievous, and notably enjoys pulling pranks on others even when in his usual slowed state. Overall, Preston has a friendly and fun-loving nature, both at regular speeds and at his typical slow pace.

characters.name: Prince Cryon
characters.description: Prince CryonNormalOne armBiographical informationAliasMr. Crayon (by Mo)GenderMaleRelationsFriendsElliott,MoEnemiesLord Kallous the Merciless(archenemy)AppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Melancholic Megalomaniac""Melancholic Megalomaniac"Prince Cryonis a supporting character inElliott from Earth,and isLord Kallous the Merciless's arch-nemesis. He is actually a clone based on the genetic material of the original Cryon, Kallous's real nemesis, who is presumed to be dead after the destruction of his and Kallous's homeworld. He first appears inMelancholic Megalomaniac. Prince Cryon is a tall, muscular blue-skinned humanoid alien with sharp eyes, a square jaw, pointed ears, a long nose, and shoulder-length white hair. In his typical outfit, he wears a brown sash with a purple gem in the middle, yellow bracers around his forearms, brown pants with a yellow trim around the waistband, yellow boots, and a yellow circlet with a purple gem matching his sash. He wields a longsword with a silver blade, a golden hilt with a red diamond in the center, a blue wrapping around the handle, and a silver pommel with a purple gem. Due to being a synthetic clone, Cryon outwardly resembles his "original" counterpart but is actually made up of different materials which have a critical weakness to water, meaning any body part that gets water on it will melt or dissolve. After Kallous becomes aware of this weakness during his reunion and battle with Cryon, it results in Cryon's left arm being dissolved and lost from the elbow down. Cryon's personality revolves around being a strong, noble, and super-masculine hero. He appears to come from a medieval-esque culture, and as a result views almost everything through that lens—for example, he believes thatMois a dragon, and immediately thinks thatElliottmust be a "damsel in distress" who needs to be rescued from it. Cryon is dedicated to the values of chivalry and strength, and is committed to vanquishing Lord Kallous. He is stubborn, dedicated, fierce, righteous, sometimes impulsive or violent, but still very deeply sworn to a code of honor and debt.

He is stubborn, dedicated, fierce, righteous, sometimes impulsive or violent, but still very deeply sworn to a code of honor and debt.

characters.name: Prince Cryon
characters.description: Prince CryonNormalOne armBiographical informationAliasMr. Crayon (by Mo)GenderMaleRelationsFriendsElliott,MoEnemiesLord Kallous the Merciless(archenemy)AppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Melancholic Megalomaniac""Melancholic Megalomaniac"Prince Cryonis a supporting character inElliott from Earth,and isLord Kallous the Merciless's arch-nemesis. He is actually a clone based on the genetic material of the original Cryon, Kallous's real nemesis, who is presumed to be dead after the destruction of his and Kallous's homeworld. He first appears inMelancholic Megalomaniac. Prince Cryon is a tall, muscular blue-skinned humanoid alien with sharp eyes, a square jaw, pointed ears, a long nose, and shoulder-length white hair. In his typical outfit, he wears a brown sash with a purple gem in the middle, yellow bracers around his forearms, brown pants with a yellow trim around the waistband, yellow boots, and a yellow circlet with a purple gem matching his sash. He wields a longsword with a silver blade, a golden hilt with a red diamond in the center, a blue wrapping around the handle, and a silver pommel with a purple gem. Due to being a synthetic clone, Cryon outwardly resembles his "original" counterpart but is actually made up of different materials which have a critical weakness to water, meaning any body part that gets water on it will melt or dissolve. After Kallous becomes aware of this weakness during his reunion and battle with Cryon, it results in Cryon's left arm being dissolved and lost from the elbow down. Cryon's personality revolves around being a strong, noble, and super-masculine hero. He appears to come from a medieval-esque culture, and as a result views almost everything through that lens—for example, he believes thatMois a dragon, and immediately thinks thatElliottmust be a "damsel in distress" who needs to be rescued from it. Cryon is dedicated to the values of chivalry and strength, and is committed to vanquishing Lord Kallous. He is stubborn, dedicated, fierce, righteous, sometimes impulsive or violent, but still very deeply sworn to a code of honor and debt.

characters.name: The Head
characters.description: The HeadBiographical informationAliasThe Head of the HiveSpeciesMany different alien species working togetherOccupationIn charge of theHiveAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Memory Mayhem""Diminishing Discourse" So, you're the anomaly that everyone's been talking about.  Tell us, what is your home planet called? The Headis a being composed of hundreds of alien specialists from different fields working together in unison, and serves as the highest authority in theHive. As a whole, the Head handles important decisions as well as the collection of information and knowledge about the Centrium, its inhabitants, and the other worlds that they come from. Due to consisting of many different individuals, the Head can lose its form or change shape (which can also be altered via a pinscreen-like device). Thetranslation gasis needed for the individuals within the Head; without it, they cannot work together to form it properly, as seen inDiminishing Discourse.

characters.name: Transport Sphere
characters.description: Transport SphereRock formSphere formBiographical informationAliasThe rockTalking space rock (byElliott)Planet of originUnknownVoiced byTheresa GallagherAppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Wednesday Part 1""Diminishing Discourse Part 1" Hello,Elliott. Hello,Frankie. Thank you for activating your transport sphere. The Transport Sphere is an object, seemingly a piece of alien technology, that transported Elliott and Frankie across space and to theCentrium. According to Frankie, it may have first landed on Earth roughly 65 million years ago. Since landing, it stayed in a dormant hibernation mode, in which it takes the form of a small rock-like object, before being accidentally activated by Frankie many years after she first discovered it in Mesozoic-age rock layers. Much of the Transport Sphere's programming, function, history, and purpose is unknown.

characters.name: Yadveer
characters.description: YadveerBiographical informationSpeciesMutualiteGenderMaleRelationsFriends105E(droid host)AppearancesFirst appearanceLatest appearance"Memory Mayhem""Memory Mayhem" Yadveer is105E'smutualite. He usually resides within her body, connected to her circuit boards. Yadveer has a symbiotic relationship with 105E, just like other mutualites with their droid hosts; in exchange for warmth from her circuit boards, he grants her the ability to project others' memories. 105E appears to appreciate him, such as when she says that not all mutualites are "as good as him". Yadveer is a small, squishy, pink blob-like creature, with six long tentacle-like limbs and a rounded heart-shaped body. His face consists of large round eyes, and a small mouth.
